title: Scientists Find Old Sea Sponge Fossil That Solves a Big Mystery
summary: Scientists have found a very old fossil of a sea sponge that helps solve a big mystery. Sea sponges are simple animals without brains or guts, and scientists thought they first lived about 700 million years ago. But the oldest clear fossils were only 540 million years old, leaving a gap of 160 million years. A team led by Shuhai Xiao discovered a 550 million-year-old sponge fossil in China. This fossil fits in the missing time and shows that early sponges might not have had hard parts, making them hard to fossilize. This discovery helps explain why we haven't found older sponge fossils and connects to questions about when animal life began on Earth.

background_read: Sea sponges are some of the oldest animals on Earth, living in oceans for millions of years. They are very simple creatures without brains or stomachs. Fossils are like nature's photographs that show us what ancient life was like. Scientists use fossils to learn about how animals evolved over time. Sometimes, there are gaps in the fossil record where we don't have fossils, which can be puzzling. This article talks about how a new fossil discovery helps fill one of those gaps for sea sponges, making it easier to understand their history.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists led by Shuhai Xiao
WHAT: Found a 550 million-year-old sea sponge fossil that solves a 160 million-year gap in the fossil record
WHERE: Along the Yangtze River in China
WHY: To explain why older sponge fossils are missing and understand when animal life began

perspectives.description: The finding of an old sponge fossil helps fill a gap in evolutionary history.
